text: So for example, if we do a mitral valve repair or replacement instead of doing the regular sternotomy where you open the breastbone, the sternum in the midline and put a retractor and

open the heart that way you can make a very small minimally invasive thoracotomy on the right side or actually extremely small incision use a video assisted approach versus a robotic approach at times use a robotic device called da Vinci we are able to cannulate the big vessels either in the groin or in the neck so
For the aortic valve we can do either an open procedure where we open the sternum and midline, either a full sternotomy or a partial sternotomy.
